# Codex Desktop for Linux

> Unofficial native Linux packaging for OpenAI Codex Desktop — DEB and AppImage builds with Wayland support.

## Project

- License: MIT
- Status: Active, source verification in CI
- Platforms: Ubuntu/Debian 22.04+ (amd64, arm64), any Linux distro via AppImage
- Repository: https://github.com/xdLawless2/codex-desktop-linux

## What It Does

Patches the upstream macOS Codex Desktop build for Linux: rebuilds native
modules, bundles the Linux Codex runtime, adds Wayland Computer Use, and
packages as DEB and AppImage.

## Installation

This repository does not publish prebuilt packages. Install the required
tooling and official Codex CLI, then download the official upstream DMG and
build locally:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/xdLawless2/codex-desktop-linux.git
cd codex-desktop-linux
npm ci
curl -fL "https://persistent.oaistatic.com/codex-app-prod/Codex.dmg" -o Codex.dmg
bash scripts/setup.sh ./Codex.dmg
npm run build:linux
```

Generated `.deb` and `.AppImage` files are written to `dist/`. See README.md
for installation and verification commands.

## Key Features

- Native .deb and AppImage packaging
- Wayland support with auto-detection
- Local DEB and AppImage builds from a user-supplied upstream DMG
- Chromium sandbox handling that fails closed
- Wayland Computer Use through xdg-desktop-portal
- System integration (desktop entry, icons, AppStream metainfo)
- Built-in `codex-desktop --doctor` diagnostics

## Important

This is an unofficial build, not affiliated with OpenAI. The repository does
not track the upstream DMG, extracted application, proprietary binaries, or
built packages in git, and it does not publish generated packages through
GitHub Releases. No unsigned APT repository or remote root installer is
published.

Computer Use is scoped to KDE Plasma 6 Wayland and documented in
docs/computer-use-linux.md.
